title: 03692_When_Personalization_Harms_Performance_Reconsidering_the_Use_of_Group_Attributes_in_Prediction
positive_examples: Formal conditions to ensure the fair use of group attributes in prediction tasks can improve model performance for specific groups.
The study highlights the need to evaluate fair use when personalizing models, which can lead to better health outcomes in clinical applications.
negative_examples: Personalization with group attributes can inadvertently reduce performance at a group level, leading to worse decisions and health outcomes.
Inaccurate predictions due to fair use violations may inflict harm, especially in clinical applications where they can lead to worse health outcomes.
